items.caption_text: Only took 130 tries to get it right! Ball had to bounce exactly right and land in the same spot from where I picked it up. Both sides of the mirror had to line up exactly. Also needed to perfectly line up my spin around at the start and end of the take.

raw.caption: Only took 130 tries to get it right! Ball had to bounce exactly right and land in the same spot from where I picked it up. Both sides of the mirror had to line up exactly. Also needed to perfectly line up my spin around at the start and end of the take.

items.caption_text:
Swipe for behind-the-scenes 🩵 new stop-motion work for LEGO Bluey.

Exploring fun and rhythmic movement is my favorite thing to do, especially when mixing characters and object physics. A big experiment on this one was switching lenses while shooting, and then blending them with post-production scaling. I think it worked really well! Keeps the single angle simplicity but adds a dynamic quality to the camera.

raw.caption:
Swipe for behind-the-scenes 🩵 new stop-motion work for LEGO Bluey.

Exploring fun and rhythmic movement is my favorite thing to do, especially when mixing characters and object physics. A big experiment on this one was switching lenses while shooting, and then blending them with post-production scaling. I think it worked really well! Keeps the single angle simplicity but adds a dynamic quality to the camera.

items.caption_text:
Comment ‘PLAY’ and I’ll DM you how to submit your (or your kid’s) ideas to finish a @LEGO short film!

LEGO Insiders Club is offering the opportunity for kids to help @aardmananimations, the creators of Wallace & Gromit and Shaun the Sheep, finish their latest short film. Ideas can be submitted in the LEGO Play app.

#ad #LEGOPartner #Boop

#Ad How I animated this fun, LEGO Minifigure loop! Comment ‘PLAY’ and I’ll send you the LEGO Play app.
